GA4 Certification Assignment

Author

Raymond Marquez

1 Module/Sub-module Completion

1.1 Figure 1: Exploration setup (show steps/segments)

1.2 Figure 2: Exploration results (show drop-offs, paths, or overlap)

2 Short Answers

2.1 What question were you trying to answer?

I was trying to determine how users move through the conversion funnel from homepage view to purchase and where the biggest drop-offs occur. I also explored which pages users commonly visit first and how navigation behavior impacts conversions.

2.2 What did you find?

  • Out of 34,852 homepage views, only 337 users (0.97%) reached the Add to Cart stage, showing a very large early funnel drop-off.

  • The homepage stage had the highest abandonment rate at 99.03%, indicating low progression into product engagement.

  • From Add to Cart to Add Payment Information, the completion rate improved to 37.09%, showing stronger intent among users who reach this stage.

  • Final purchases totaled 85 users, meaning only about 0.2% of initial homepage visitors completed the funnel.

2.3 What’s your next investigation step based on the results?

Next, I would analyze landing page performance and traffic source segmentation to identify which channels or pages produce higher Add to Cart rates. I would also review device type or user behavior flow to determine where engagement drops sharply.

2.4 What’s one action you would recommend for your CEP/client?

  • Optimize homepage design with clearer calls-to-action and product visibility to reduce the 99% early abandonment rate.

  • Implement A/B testing on homepage layouts or featured products to increase Add to Cart conversion.
